conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture on similar growth of $N_{DFL}(\varepsilon)$ and $1/\delta_{TRL}(\varepsilon)$
summary: The conjecture that $N_{DFL}(\varepsilon)$ and $1/\delta_{TRL}(\varepsilon)$ have similar growth remains open as of May 2026. No follow-up paper resolving or substantially advancing this informal conjecture was found in the literature. The gap between the known lower bound $(1/\varepsilon)^{c\log(1/\varepsilon)}$ (from the Behrend construction) and the upper bound tower$(O(\log(1/\varepsilon)))$ (Fox) remains unresolved. A 2025 paper (arXiv:2507.05231) improves the constant in the lower-bound construction for the triangle removal lemma but does not address the specific comparison between $N_{DFL}$ and $1/\delta_{TRL}$.
search_queries: Fox Zhao "removal lemmas" "approximate homomorphisms" 2104.11626 N_DFL delta_TRL growth
arxiv 2104.11626 Fox Zhao "triangle removal lemma" "Behrend" tower bound similar growth
Fox Zhao "approximate homomorphisms" "dense graph" "removal" N_DFL delta_TRL subexponential 2022 2023 2024 follow-up
"triangle-free lemma" "removal lemma" bounds tower subexponential 2023 2024 2025 Fox Zhao arxiv
notes: No follow-up found that directly addresses the conjecture. The paper arXiv:2507.05231 ('An improved construction for the triangle removal lemma', 2025) is tangentially related—it improves the constant in the lower-bound construction showing $1/\delta_{TRL}(\varepsilon) \geq (1/\varepsilon)^{C_{\rm new}\log_2(1/\varepsilon)}$—but does not address the comparison with $N_{DFL}(\varepsilon)$. The conjecture is phrased informally ('We suspect') and is likely a long-horizon open problem given the difficulty of the existing bounds.
